r. B. Bryan, superintendent of
public instruction, has issued cir
cular to county school superinten-
dents, calling especial attention to
changes in schoo! laws made by the
last legislature with reference to
teachers certificate regulations.
Briefly, the laws now provide that
any holder of first grade certifi-
cate who has taught not less than
80 months. SO of them in this state,
shall be granted permanent first
grade certificate on presenting salts
factory evidence and application to
the county superintendent. Exam
nations for life diplomas and GER
tificates are to be held in May and
August of each year in counties des-
ignated by the state Office. School
board examination meetings in June
are abolished. After May all APP
plicants for first grade certificates
must qualify in physicai geography
in addition to present branches.
Holders of second grade certificates
may be granted first grades if per-
centages in 9 previous examinations
have not fallen below 90 and does
not fall below the minimum in the
succeeding examination on addi-
tional subjects. Applicants for first
grade certificates ave exempt from
examination on subjects in which
they have stood 80 per cent. Credits
on the May or previous eXamina-
tons cannot be taken advantage of,
however, as the law does not go
into effect until June.
